channel.title: Wasil Zafar — Psychology
channel.description: Latest psychology articles from Wasil Zafar

item.title: Foundations of Social Psychology
item.description: Explore the foundations of social psychology — learn what it is, how it differs from other disciplines, key research methods, classic experiments like Milgram and Asch, and ethical guidelines.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: The Self-Concept & Identity
item.description: Explore the self-concept in social psychology — understand actual, ideal, and ought selves, self-discrepancy theory, identity formation, impression management, and cultural influences on self-construal.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Self-Esteem & Self-Perception
item.description: Master self-esteem and self-perception in social psychology — contingent self-worth, narcissism, Bem
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Social Cognition
item.description: Master social cognition — learn how schemas, heuristics, and cognitive biases shape the way we perceive, interpret, and remember information about other people and social situations.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Attribution Theory
item.description: Master attribution theory in social psychology — learn how we explain behavior through internal and external causes, the fundamental attribution error, actor-observer bias, and self-serving attributions.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Cognitive Dissonance
item.description: Master cognitive dissonance theory — Leon Festinger
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Conformity & Obedience
item.description: Explore conformity and obedience in social psychology — Asch
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Compliance & Persuasion
item.description: Master compliance techniques and persuasion psychology — Cialdini
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Social Influence in Groups
item.description: Explore social influence within groups — social facilitation, social loafing, minority influence, emotional contagion, and how the presence of others impacts individual performance and behavior.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Social Identity Theory
item.description: Master Social Identity Theory — Henri Tajfel
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Stereotypes, Prejudice & Discrimination
item.description: Understand stereotypes, prejudice, and discrimination — cognitive foundations, emotional prejudice, behavioral discrimination, implicit bias, the Implicit Association Test, and modern forms of racism.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Stereotype Threat & Reducing Prejudice
item.description: Learn about stereotype threat and evidence-based strategies for reducing prejudice — contact hypothesis, cooperative learning, shared identity, Robbers Cave experiment, and recategorization.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Group Decision Making & Groupthink
item.description: Understand group decision making and groupthink — Irving Janis
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Deindividuation & Bystander Effect
item.description: Explore deindividuation and the bystander effect — how anonymity in crowds changes behavior, diffusion of responsibility, Kitty Genovese, leadership in groups, and strategies for conflict resolution.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Attraction & Relationships
item.description: Explore the psychology of attraction and relationships — proximity, similarity, physical attractiveness, Sternberg
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Aggression & Prosocial Behavior
item.description: Explore aggression and prosocial behavior — biological and social causes of aggression, frustration-aggression hypothesis, media violence, altruism, empathy-altruism hypothesis, and helping behavior.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Culture, Socialization & Media
item.description: Explore how culture, socialization, and media shape social behavior — individualism vs collectivism, agents of socialization, social media psychology, echo chambers, and digital identity.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Applied Social Psychology
item.description: Explore applied social psychology — self-fulfilling prophecy in education, cognitive dissonance in therapy, persuasion in marketing, behavior change in public policy, nudging, and social norms interventions.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Advanced Topics & Modern Research
item.description: Explore advanced social psychology — social neuroscience, behavioral economics, digital behavior, collective action, moral psychology, and the future of the field.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Research Methods & Academic Mastery
item.description: Master research methods in social psychology — experimental design, hypothesis formulation, statistical analysis, the replication crisis, and writing research papers for academic publication.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Cognitive Psychology Series Part 14: Computational & AI Models of Cognition
item.description: Customize Settings Cookie Preferences Essential Cookies (Required) Necessary for the website to function properly. item.title: Practical Applications - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 8
item.description: Behavioral psychology isn't just academic—it's a practical toolkit for improving life, work, and health. In this eighth part of our series, we apply everything we've learned to real-world contexts. The best behavioral interventions combine multiple principles: make behavior easy (friction
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Behavior Change Models - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 6
item.description: Behavior change models provide systematic frameworks for understanding why people behave as they do and how to design effective interventions. In this sixth part of our series, we explore the major theoretical models. Behavior change models help identify which factors to target and which
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Decision-Making Psychology - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 3
item.description: Humans are not rational calculators—we use mental shortcuts and are influenced by emotions. In this third part of our series, we explore the psychology behind decision-making. Bounded rationality means we make "good enough" decisions with limited information and cognitive resources—not optimal ones.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Foundations of Behavior - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 1
item.description: Behavioral psychology studies observable actions and the environmental factors that influence them. In this first part of our comprehensive 11-part series, we'll explore the fundamental concepts that underpin all behavioral science. Behavior is fundamentally the interaction between a person and
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Habit Formation & Breaking - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 2
item.description: Habits are automatic behaviors stored in the basal ganglia. In this second part of our comprehensive 11-part series, we'll explore how habits form, persist, and change. Habits are not about motivation—they're about repeated loops that become automatic through consistent practice.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Motivation & Drive - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 4
item.description: Motivation is the internal and external force that initiates, guides, and sustains goal-oriented behavior. In this fourth part of our series, we dive deep into the psychology of drive. Motivation is not a fixed trait—it's a dynamic process influenced by goals, rewards, autonomy, and meaning.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Behavioral Neuroscience Basics - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 9
item.description: Understanding the neuroscience behind behavior gives us deeper insights into why we act the way we do and how to design more effective interventions. In this ninth part of our series, we explore the brain-behavior connection. The brain evolved to conserve energy—habits form because automated
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Nudge Theory & Choice Architecture: Behavioral Economics in Action
item.description: A nudge is any aspect of choice architecture that alters people's behavior predictably without forbidding options or significantly changing economic incentives. In this fifth part of our series, we explore the science of behavioral design. Nudges work by making desired behaviors easier, more
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Behavioral Research Methods - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 10
item.description: Understanding research methods helps you critically evaluate behavioral claims and design your own interventions. In this tenth part of our series, we explore the scientific toolkit of behavioral psychology. Correlation ≠ causation. Only well-designed experiments with random assignment can
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Social Influence & Persuasion - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 7
item.description: Humans are fundamentally social beings—our behaviors, beliefs, and decisions are profoundly shaped by others. In this seventh part of our series, we explore the science of social influence and persuasion. Social influence operates through three main routes: compliance (behavioral change),
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Applied Behavioral Therapy - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 11
item.description: Behavioral therapy applies the principles we've learned throughout this series to clinical treatment. In this final part of our series, we explore evidence-based therapeutic approaches rooted in behavioral psychology. Behavioral therapies focus on changing what people do (behaviors) rather than
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Behavioral Psychology Assessment
item.description: Test your mastery of the Behavioral Psychology series. All questions, no time limit, detailed feedback after each answer. This assessment covers all topics from the Behavioral Psychology series.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Cognitive Psychology Assessment
item.description: Test your mastery of the Cognitive Psychology series. All questions, no time limit, detailed feedback after each answer. This assessment covers all topics from the Cognitive Psychology series.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Social Psychology Assessment
item.description: Test your mastery of the Social Psychology series. All questions, no time limit, detailed feedback after each answer. This assessment covers all topics from the Social Psychology series.
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)
